item.title: Nancy Guthrie video may have been erased after FBI kept out for days, director says
item.description: FBI Director Kash Patel says the four-day delay in federal involvement may have cost crucial Nest doorbell footage that was on the verge of deletion.
item.content:encoded:
EXCLUSIVE: The delay in allowing the FBI to assist in the investigation into Nancy Guthrie's suspected abduction may have cost crucial video evidence due to the elaborate process involved in recovering Nest doorbell footage from the crime scene when the victim did not have a cloud subscription and her physical camera was missing as well, according to the FBI director.

Guthrie, the mother of "Today" co-host Savannah Guthrie, was taken from her Tucson, Arizona, home in the early hours of Feb. 1, according to authorities.

The FBI worked with Google to recover cached video from Guthrie's Nest camera that was not intended for long-term storage and was on the verge of deletion, Director Kash Patel told Fox News Digital.

If there was more, it was lost before the FBI became involved, and its evidentiary value remains unknown.

NANCY GUTHRIE SHERIFF SCOLDED COUNTY OFFICIAL FOR SHARING TIP WITH FBI, SHE CLAIMS IN INTERVIEW

"We were looking for the needle of all needles in all haystacks, because since she didn't pay for it, they didn't record that data," he said. "But there was a chance that it was located in their cache just before it was being deleted."

That's where investigators found the video and still images that Patel made public on Feb. 10, in the hope that someone would recognize the suspect and turn him in.

A short time later, FBI experts had generated a rough physical description. The masked suspect is described as between 5 feet, 9 inches and 5 feet, 10 inches tall with a medium build. He was wearing a black Ozark Trail backpack.

NANCY GUTHRIE CASE: FORMER COP SHOWS HOW MISSING MOM COULD VANISH JUST YARDS FROM HOMES

A masked man appeared on Guthrie's Nest camera at least twice — once on the day of her Feb. 1 abduction, and once about three weeks earlier, Fox News Digital has reported previously. Authorities have not publicly ruled out the potential for additional suspects in the case.

There is only a single still image publicly available from the earlier visit. Experts say it's likely that video existed at one point.

If the FBI were involved sooner, agents may have been able to recover additional evidence, which potentially may have included a suspect vehicle, more footage of the masked intruder or even additional imagery from a prior visit to Guthrie's home, Patel said.

NANCY GUTHRIE SHERIFF DEFENDS PACE OF INVESTIGATION MORE THAN 4 MONTHS INTO SEARCH

"What we do know is that the longer you wait, there's no chance of recovering the video," said Morgan Wright, the CEO and founder of the National Center for Open and Unsolved Cases. "The earlier you get started, the better your chances. Waiting four days may have been fatal to the recovery of some video that could have been more illuminating."

FOLLOW THE FOX TRUE CRIME TEAM ON X

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST TRUE CRIME NEWS

Because Google and the FBI were able to recover at least some video through this process, it's fair to infer that earlier video would have existed as well, Wright told Fox News Digital.

WATCH: Nancy Guthrie's Nest doorbell cam records masked suspect

SEND US A TIP

"If this was recovered from a backend cache and it was almost deleted, that implies that another video was deleted because they would have been able to recover more than what they did," he said. "Timing is always an issue."

At this point, however, whatever may or may not have existed remains unknown and unavailable to investigators.

"Time is the enemy of all investigations and the thief of vital evidence," Wright said. "You can never go back and get this evidence once it's gone. Time is the most critical factor in any investigation whether it's finding the suspect, finding the victim, or finding the evidence."

Patel has previously said the FBI was ready to help on day one and explained this week that he wanted to deploy scores of FBI personnel from the outset. However, local authorities didn't request the FBI's assistance for four days.

LISTEN TO THE NEW 'CRIME & JUSTICE WITH DONNA ROTUNNO' PODCAST

By the time the FBI arrived, the crime scene had already been released. People had been allowed to knock on Guthrie's front door, where her blood was on the porch. It was secured once more, and FBI agents spent hours examining the home and the yard, collecting additional home security cameras.

Pima County Sheriff Chris Nanos has countered that an FBI task force member was present during the initial stages of the investigation — however that officer is not a federal agent.

The sheriff's department declined to comment for this story.

Anyone with information on Guthrie's kidnapping is asked to dial 1-800-CALL-FBI or 520-88-CRIME to remain anonymous.

The combined sum of rewards in the case is more than $1.2 million.

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: ESPN to part ways with Cam Newton as part of talent layoffs that also included Ryan Clark
item.description: Cam Newton is out at ESPN as part of layoffs, leaving Stephen A. Smith without his primary Monday co-host on "First Take" during football season.
item.content:encoded:
ESPN is parting ways with Cam Newton, multiple sources told OutKick.

The network will include Newton in a round of layoffs that could be announced as soon as Tuesday. OutKick reported Monday that NFL analyst Ryan Clark would be among the on-air employees let go.

ESPN featured Newton over the past two seasons as a debate partner for Stephen A. Smith on "First Take."

Last season, the network elevated Newton to replace Shannon Sharpe as Smith's primary Monday co-host during football season, the show's highest-rated day of the week.

Sources say ESPN will look for a permanent replacement for Newton on the Monday editions of the show.

Newton and Smith engaged in a public back-and-forth last spring after Newton invited Jason Whitlock, whom Smith has said he "hates with a passion," onto his podcast.

Smith criticized Newton for not defending him when Whitlock cited what he described as fabrications in Smith's 2023 memoir.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

Newton responded that he didn't challenge Whitlock because, in his view, there was "nothing to correct."

In any event, Smith will be without his two main analysts during the football season in Newton and Clark.

This is a breaking news story. Check back to OutKick for more news and analysis.

item.title: Ed Harris says he was ready to leave Dutton Ranch after feeling misled about his role
item.description: Ed Harris says his early experience on "Dutton Ranch" was so bad he wanted out, claiming his role as Everett McKinney wasn't what he was promised.
item.content:encoded:
Ed Harris didn't hold back when talking about his experience working on "Dutton Ranch."

The "Yellowstone" spinoff from Taylor Sheridan became an instant hit on Paramount+ when season one premiered. It felt like the perfect continuation of the Dutton saga.

Harris stars in the show as veterinarian and war veteran Everett McKinney – a man with a kind heart and violent capabilities.

DUTTON RANCH STAR NATALIE ALYN LIND DROPS BEHIND THE SCENES PHOTOS AHEAD OF SEASON FINALE THIS FRIDAY

Everett was one of my favorite characters in season one, but it turns out Harris didn't have a great time filming the show.

Harris revealed in an interview with Variety that his early experience on the show was so bad that he was ready to say, "Get me the f** out of here."

"Prior to signing on for it, I was talked to quite a bit about what the season was, and what my character was going to do and that I was one of the four main characters. And that wasn’t really the case," Harris said in an interview with Variety.

TAYLOR SHERIDAN SHOCKS 'YELLOWSTONE' FANS WITH NEW SPINOFF SERIES, PROVIDES VIEWERS WITH DARK RIDE

He explained that when watching the season that he realized his character was "relatively important to the story but the doing of it did not feel that way."

The popular actor also teased an expanded role in season two with "a little bit more to do."

You can watch his full comments below, and let me know your thoughts at David.Hookstead@outkick.com.

TAYLOR SHERIDAN SAYS HE DOESN'T CARE WHAT CRITICS THINK, ADMITS TO 'RAGE BAITING' THEM ON PURPOSE

Well, in terms of being blunt and honest, good luck finding someone in Hollywood who gives more straightforward comments.

I think it's safe to say Harris was very disappointed with how the situation unfolded behind the scenes. The interesting thing is the fact that Everett definitely feels like a major character.

Top four?

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

Probably not. Key to the story? Absolutely.

Despite my assessment of the situation, it's clear that Harris didn't have that impression while filming the series.

Now, the question is how will things change in season two for Everett's storyline? I hope some big things are on the way. Let me know what you think about Harris' comments at David.Hookstead@outkick.com.

item.title: CNN legal analyst calls out 'utterly ridiculous' claim by Mamdani about Netanyahu arrest
item.description: CNN legal analyst Elie Honig called Zohran Mamdani's suggestion that he could arrest Benjamin Netanyahu "utterly ridiculous" and lacking any legal basis.
item.content:encoded:
CNN legal analyst Elie Honig called out New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani on Monday for saying he was looking at whether the city's authorities could arrest Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu if he visits.

"So, Elie, give us a reality check here. Can Mamdani have Netanyahu arrested?" CNN's Brianna Keilar asked Honig.

"Absolutely not. It is utterly ridiculous for the mayor of New York City to even suggest that he might have the authority to order the arrest of Benjamin Netanyahu or any visiting foreign head of state. First of all, the Mayor of New York City has zero law enforcement authority. He does not tell the Manhattan D.A., the NYPD, who to arrest, who to indict," Honig responded.

Honig noted that the U.S. was not a signatory to the International Criminal Court (ICC), and therefore the arrest warrant the ICC issued for Netanyahu has no impact here.

MAMDANI’S ‘DERANGED’ ISRAEL TAKE RESURFACES AS HE TARGETS NETANYAHU

"Second of all, this warrant that we're talking about here was issued by the ICC, the International Criminal Court, which we are not signatories to here in the United States. So, that warrant, issued by the ICC, has zero legal effect here in the United States. In fact, we have a federal law here in the U.S., passed 24 years ago, that says, if necessary, the president can send military troops to pull our people and our allies out of the ICC. So, that warrant has zero impact," he said.

Honig added, "The Constitution is quite clear that the conduct of foreign diplomacy, foreign affairs, is entirely up to the federal government. It's certainly not up to local and county governments in New York City or elsewhere."

"So, I know the mayor is having his best lawyers look at this right now. Let me save them some time. You do not have the authority to do this," the CNN legal analyst concluded.

In remarks first reported by The New York Times, Mamdani called Netanyahu a "war criminal" who "belongs in The Hague" and said his administration is in an "active conversation" with the city's Law Department over what legal authority New York City possesses should the Israeli prime minister travel to the Big Apple.

"Whatever the law allows me to do in New York City, that’s what we will do," Mamdani said, adding his administration would not write new laws for that purpose.

NETANYAHU FIRES BACK AT NYC MAYOR MAMDANI'S VOW TO ENFORCE 'BOGUS ARREST WARRANT'

When reached for comment by Fox News Digital, the mayor's office referenced Mamdani's statement from an unrelated press conference on Monday.

"When it comes to Benjamin Netanyahu, we are talking about someone who's the subject of an international criminal court arrest warrant," Mamdani said. "And he's the subject of the arrest warrant for alleged crimes against humanity for war crimes, and he's the architect of the genocide of Palestinians in Gaza as Israel's prime minister. These are not facts of my personal assessment; they are facts of public record. And I believe that when someone is charged with an international criminal court warrant, it's something that should be taken seriously, whether it's Benjamin Netanyahu or it's Vladamir Putin or whomever else." 

"What I've also made clear is my administration will follow all applicable local laws," Mamdani said. "That is our commitment, that is something we will follow through on when it comes to adherence to local law and that's all I have to add on that." 

 He continued, "My point is that if someone is charged with a warrant by the international criminal court for these kinds of crimes, that's something that I believe should be honored, and I've also said that we will follow our local laws."

CLICK HERE TO FOLLOW FOX NEWS MEDIA AND CULTURE ON FACEBOOK

U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz slammed Mamdani's remarks as "pure political theater," saying the mayor has no legal authority to arrest Netanyahu.

In a post on X, Waltz argued that the U.S. is not party to the ICC's founding treaty, the U.N. Headquarters Agreement protects visiting heads of government, head-of-government immunity applies, and "federal authority trumps any local mayor's wishes."

The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and former Defense Minister Yoav Gallant in November 2024, over alleged war crimes and crimes against humanity tied to Israel's military campaign in Gaza.

item.title: WNBA All-Star charity initiative supports sports group that says it is ‘moving beyond the binary of girl’
item.description: A WNBA All-Star beneficiary says it is "moving beyond the binary of 'girl'" while the league frames the $100,000 prize pool as keeping girls in sports.
item.content:encoded:
The WNBA is celebrating its milestone 30th season with a new All-Star initiative that the league says is designed to help keep girls involved in sports.

But one of the Chicago-area organizations selected to benefit from the league’s $100,000 All-Star prize pool says on its own website that it is "moving beyond the binary of ‘girl’" and welcomes "gender-expansive participants regardless of gender identity or expression."

The organization is Girls in the Game, the charity affiliated with "Team Spoon" in the 2026 WNBA All-Star Game.

According to the league’s July 15 release, the WNBA All-Star teams will compete for a $100,000 prize pool benefiting two Chicago-area organizations "dedicated to keeping girls involved in sports." The winning team’s organization receives $70,000, while the runner-up’s organization gets $30,000. Team Coop is playing for Angels Athletics, while Team Spoon is supporting Girls in the Game.

MEGAN RAPINOE'S LATEST DEFENSE OF MALES IN WOMEN'S SPORTS COLLAPSES UNDER ITS OWN HYPOCRISY

The WNBA described Girls in the Game as an organization that "partners with schools, parks, and community centers in Chicago to offer sports, health, and leadership programs."

That sounds straightforward enough until you dig deeper into the Girls in the Game website.

OutKick/Fox News Digital reviewed the organization’s website and found language that raises obvious questions about how Girls in the Game defines "girl."

Under the heading "We stand for inclusivity," Girls in the Game says: "Moving beyond the binary of ‘girl’, we welcome LGBTQ+ youth and gender-expansive participants regardless of gender identity or expression."

Wait, what?

Girls in the Game is not a generic youth charity that happens to work with girls. The organization repeatedly frames its mission around girls, girls’ sports and girls-only spaces. So when it says it is "moving beyond the binary of ‘girl,’" people deserve to know what that actually means.

OutKick/Fox News Digital has not been able to confirm whether any biological males have participated in Girls in the Game’s girl-centered sports programming.

According to its 2025 annual report, Girls in the Game served 3,059 participants in FY25. The report says its Chicago After School program alone served 921 participants and girls "play sports, learn about healthy lifestyle choices, and improve their leadership skills in an all-girl setting." The report also says the program provides a "safe, non-competitive environment for girls to learn and be physically active."

The organization’s public-facing program materials do not clearly state whether participation is based on biological sex, gender identity or another eligibility standard.

But Girls in the Game’s own LGBTQ resource pages also point users toward gender-identity-related services. One page lists Howard Brown as a resource that includes "gender-affirming health care" and "trans and non-binary care," along with a support group intended for "BIPOC, trans, non-conforming, questioning individuals" and the Trans Lifeline hotline.

Another Girls in the Game LGBTQ resource page lists "Gender Affirming clothing, makeup, community resources," along with God’s Closet, which it describes as offering "clothes and tailoring for gender-nonconforming/trans people." That page also lists a support group for "BIPOC trans, non-conforming, questioning individuals" and Trans Lifeline.

MARTINA NAVRATILOVA SAYS SHE TRIED TO WARN KAMALA HARRIS' CAMPAIGN ABOUT TRANS ATHLETES: 'NOBODY WOULD LISTEN'

If the WNBA is promoting this as an initiative to keep girls involved in sports, and if one of the beneficiaries says it is moving beyond the binary definition of "girl," then the league and the organization need to explain who is eligible for those programs.

OutKick/Fox News Digital reached out to Girls in the Game and asked whether biological males who "identify" as girls, transgender girls, "nonbinary" or "gender-expansive" are eligible to participate in its girl-centered sports programming.

OutKick/Fox News Digital also asked whether any Girls in the Game programs operate as girls-only spaces, whether those programs are limited based on biological sex or gender identity, whether parents are told before enrollment that the organization is "moving beyond the binary of ‘girl,’" and whether the WNBA All-Star money would be unrestricted or directed toward a specific program.

Girls in the Game did not respond to multiple requests for comment.

OutKick/Fox News Digital also contacted Angels Athletics, the organization affiliated with Team Coop, to ask whether its girls basketball and girls sports programs are limited to biological girls or open based on gender identity. Angels Athletics also did not respond to multiple requests for comment.

Then there’s the WNBA.

OutKick/Fox News Digital asked the league whether it was aware of Girls in the Game’s "moving beyond the binary of ‘girl’" language before selecting the organization for the All-Star initiative.

OutKick/Fox News Digital also asked whether the league believes an organization that says it is moving beyond the binary definition of "girl" fits an initiative described as keeping girls involved in sports, whether the WNBA asked Girls in the Game about eligibility for biological males who identify as girls or "gender-expansive" participants, and whether the WNBA supports biological males participating in girls’ youth sports programming if they "identify" as girls.

The WNBA did not respond to OutKick/Fox News Digital’s request for comment.

Maybe there’s a simple explanation.

Girls in the Game might argue that its programming is non-competitive or centered on youth development rather than formal leagues. But that wouldn’t answer the important question. The WNBA did not announce a prize-pool initiative to help keep "gender-expansive" youth involved in sports. It said the money would benefit organizations dedicated to keeping girls involved in sports.

Girls in the Game, meanwhile, describes some of its own programming as taking place in an "all-girl setting," while also saying it is "moving beyond the binary of ‘girl’" and welcomes participants regardless of gender identity or expression.

EDUCATION SECRETARY LINDA MCMAHON RIPS CALIFORNIA TRANS ATHLETE ‘COMPROMISE,’ TELLS NEWSOM TO ‘PICK A SIDE’

Whether the sports are competitive or non-competitive is not the only issue. The issue is that an organization built around girls’ sports spaces is openly advertising that those spaces are not necessarily just for girls.

As for the WNBA, maybe the league will say it selected the organization for its broader youth-development work and never asked about eligibility because the money is not tied to competitive girls’ sports.

These are not difficult questions. This whole mess could be cleaned up with a simple email response. Instead, Girls in the Game, Angels Athletics and the WNBA have not provided one.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

The WNBA is using its All-Star Game, one of its biggest events of the season, to promote an initiative framed around "keeping girls involved in sports."

The league wants credit for helping keep girls in sports, a noble endeavor to be sure.

But before the commissioner’s office pats itself on the back too hard, it should probably explain what it means by "girls."

item.title: Coffee lovers can take heart: Up to 5 cups a day appears safe for most adults, experts say
item.description: The American Heart Association says up to 400 milligrams of caffeine daily may lower risk of heart disease, stroke and Type 2 diabetes — with a key caveat.
item.content:encoded:
Coffee drinkers may not have to feel guilty about reaching for another cup.

A new scientific statement from the American Heart Association says that consuming up to 400 milligrams of caffeine a day — the equivalent of about five 8-ounce cups of black coffee — is generally safe for most healthy adults and may even be linked to a lower risk of heart disease, stroke, heart failure and Type 2 diabetes. The findings were published Monday in the journal Circulation.

The review also found that the potential benefits are most consistently seen with plain black coffee rather than beverages loaded with sugar, flavored syrups or heavy cream.

SCIENTISTS USED ELECTRICITY ON COFFEE AND DISCOVERED WHAT MAKES IT TASTE GOOD

"For most healthy adults, up to about 400 milligrams of caffeine a day, which is roughly five cups of black coffee, appears safe," South Carolina-based registered dietitian Lauren Manaker, who was not involved in the research, told Fox News Digital.

"And the latest research links moderate coffee drinking with a lower risk of heart disease, stroke, heart failure and Type 2 diabetes. That's reassuring news for coffee lovers."

Manaker cautioned, however, that what people add to their coffee matters.

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR OUR LIFESTYLE NEWSLETTER

"How you take your coffee makes a real difference," she said. "The benefits show up most clearly with plain black coffee. Once you start piling on sugar, flavored syrups and cream, you may be trading away some of the good you were hoping to get."

The American Heart Association also emphasized that coffee should not be lumped together with energy drinks, which often contain much higher doses of caffeine and other stimulants.

Higher amounts of caffeine — particularly from energy drinks and energy shots — have been associated with elevated blood pressure and irregular heart rhythms.

"Coffee and energy drinks are not the same thing, and we shouldn't treat them that way," Manaker said.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE LIFESTYLE NEWS

"Energy drinks and shots can pack three to four times the caffeine of regular coffee, often with added ingredients, and higher doses have been linked to raised blood pressure and irregular heart rhythms. That's the source I'd steer people away from."

The researchers also noted that caffeine doesn't affect everyone equally. Factors including age, medications, genetics and how quickly the body metabolizes caffeine can all influence an individual's tolerance and risk.

TEST YOURSELF WITH OUR LATEST LIFESTYLE QUIZ

More research is still needed, particularly because much of the existing evidence is based on observational studies.

"There's no one-size-fits-all caffeine limit," Manaker said. "Your age, your medications, your genetics and how quickly your body clears caffeine all shape how you respond. What feels fine for your co-worker might leave you jittery or awake at 2 a.m."

item.title: Florida man arrested in IHOP restaurant after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver
item.description: Jovaughn Nathan Matthie was arrested in the middle of an IHOP restaurant after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver nearby. He has been charged with attempted murder.
item.content:encoded:
A Florida man was arrested Monday inside an IHOP restaurant in Orlando after police say he stabbed a rideshare driver.

Jovaughn Nathan Matthie allegedly stabbed the driver after the individual "refused to provide a ride without a request through the rideshare app," according to the Orlando Police Department.

Police have not released the driver’s condition. Matthie has been charged with attempted murder.

After the stabbing, Matthie fled to a nearby IHOP in northeastern Orlando, police said.

DHS SEEKS CUSTODY OF ILLEGAL IMMIGRANT CHARGED WITH MURDER AFTER TRUMP DEPORTATION, BIDEN RELEASE

Body camera footage released by the department showed multiple officers surrounding Matthie as he sat and drank a hot beverage.

The restaurant appeared to have been cleared out, as there were no other customers around, according to the footage.

Officers repeatedly told Matthie to get on the ground, lie on his stomach and place his hands behind his back.

FLORIDA EXECUTES 74-YEAR-OLD FOR WIFE'S MURDER, BECOMING OLDEST INMATE PUT TO DEATH IN STATE'S MODERN HISTORY

When Matthie was slow to put his hands behind his back, an officer holding a police dog warned that he would be "dog bit" if he did not comply.

After he was handcuffed, two officers got him to his feet before noticing he had a "long" object in his pocket, according to the video.

Another officer carefully reached inside Matthie's sweatpants and found an item he identified as a knife wrapped in white cloth.

The Orlando Police Department has not released additional details, saying it is withholding certain information to "protect the integrity of the investigation."

item.title: Utah hiker killed by lightning just steps from mountain summit during trek with brother
item.description: A lightning strike near the summit of Salt Lake County's highest peak killed a 59-year-old hiker whose brother watched him collapse just steps away.
item.content:encoded:
A Utah man hiking with his brother was killed last week after a lightning bolt struck him just steps from the summit of the highest peak in Salt Lake County, authorities said.

Craig Nelson, 59, set out Saturday morning with his brother for the summit of the east peak of American Fork Twin Peaks after the pair rode the Snowbird tram partway up the mountain. The brothers had made the hike together several times before, Salt Lake County Sheriff Rosie Rivera said during a news conference on Monday.

Rivera said that around 10:30 a.m. the brothers stopped to eat lunch and noticed the weather was beginning to change. The surviving brother later told investigators they watched the sky and, because they never saw any lightning, decided to keep going.

HIKER WHO SET OUT IN WARM SPRING WEATHER FOUND DEAD AFTER SNOWSTORM IN NEW HAMPSHIRE MOUNTAINS

"As we know in Utah, the weather changes very frequently," Rivera said, later adding that the weather is "very unpredictable."

About an hour later, the brother reached the east peak first and turned around to head back down after noticing the weather had worsened, Rivera said. Nelson continued toward the summit. The brother told investigators that he saw Nelson suddenly collapse about 20 feet away from him and believed he had been struck by lightning.

The brother rushed back toward the Snowbird tram until he regained cellphone service and called 911 shortly after noon.

RESCUERS FREE CLIMBER TRAPPED BENEATH 16,000-POUND BOULDER ON OREGON'S MOUNT HOOD IN COMPLEX OPERATION

A Utah Department of Public Safety helicopter quickly located Nelson near the summit, but rescuers had to wait for the storm to move through before they could safely reach him. He was pronounced dead at the scene. Snowbird Ski Patrol later helped escort Nelson's brother off the mountain, according to the Salt Lake County Sheriff's Search and Rescue team.

American Fork Twin Peaks rises to 11,461 feet and is the highest point in Salt Lake County. Rivera emphasized that mountain weather can change with little warning and urged hikers to check the forecast before heading out. She said hikers should leave exposed ridgelines and summits as soon as storms begin moving in.

The sheriff's office also urged hikers to move to lower ground, stay away from isolated trees and bodies of water, and, if they cannot reach shelter, crouch low with their feet together until the storm passes.

The Salt Lake County Sheriff's Office said the investigation remains open while the Utah medical examiner determines Nelson's official cause of death.

item.title: Blue state mocked after 'Sex and the City' actress tapped to recommend top judges
item.description: Cynthia Nixon's appointment to New York's Commission on Judicial Nomination draws mockery from lawmakers who question her legal qualifications.
item.content:encoded:
New York State is drawing mockery after the chief judge of its highest court appointed "Sex and the City" actress Cynthia Nixon to a commission that screens and recommends candidates for the state's top judicial posts.

Nixon, who played attorney Miranda Hobbes on the 2000s hit series, will be one of four members of the 12-seat Commission on Judicial Nomination that are required to not be licensed members of the New York State Bar to practice law in the Empire State.

She was appointed Thursday by Chief Judge Rowan Wilson.

"Only in Kathy Hochul’s New York could playing a lawyer on HBO qualify you to pick the judges on our state’s highest court," Rep. Michael Lawler, R-N.Y., told Fox News Digital on Monday.

DEMOCRATIC SOCIALIST ACTRESS CYNTHIA NIXON HANDED POWERFUL ROLE IN HELPING CHOOSE NEW YORK TOP JUDGES

"Selecting far-left activist actress Cynthia Nixon to help select New York’s top judges will not bring ‘Justice In The City,’" remarked the National Review’s John Fund in a tweet.

George Washington University law professor Jonathan Turley added on X that the move is the latest in a pattern wherein the "New York legal system continues to make a parody of itself."

SOCIALISM’S RISE INSIDE THE DEMOCRATIC PARTY NOW THREATENS THE AMERICAN DREAM

Other online critics mocked the fact Nixon’s character was a lawyer but that she is not one in real life.

One posted an image of "ER" actor Noah Wyle – who played a doctor -- riffing that he could be the next Surgeon General. "I played the part of an astronaut in a school play when I was maybe 10 years old. Should I prepare my resume for NASA?" another wrote.

Saritha Komatireddy, the Republican challenging state Attorney General Letitia James, said that appointing a "celebrity activist with zero legal background to help shape the future of our judiciary sends the wrong message entirely."

SOCIALIST SURGE: MAMDANI FLEXES GROWING POLITICAL MUSCLE AS HE TAKES ON DEM ESTABLISHMENT

"This is part of a broader trend in New York: politics increasingly taking precedence over public safety and common sense," Komatireddy told the New York Post.

Nixon is aligned with the Democratic Socialists of America and unsuccessfully ran for governor in 2018 in a primary against Andrew Cuomo.

She campaigned for former Mayor Bill de Blasio in 2013 and current Mayor Zohran Mamdani in 2025.

At least one Democrat also sounded off against Nixon’s appointment, appearing to blame farther-left members of his party for dispensing with Gov. Kathy Hochul’s original nominee to Rowan’s seat.

"The price New Yorkers will pay for the bigots and haters who took out the immensely qualified Justice LaSalle is that Cynthia Nixon now gets to vet the judiciary. Excelsior!" tweeted Assemb. Kalman Yeger, D-Borough Park, riffing on the state’s "Ever Upward" slogan at the end.

Hochul settled on Wilson after New York State Senate Democrats torpedoed her nomination of fellow Democrat Hector LaSalle to lead the court’s highest bench for appearing too conservative and amid opposition from activist and labor groups.

Republicans will also get their shot at appointing a member to the commission alongside Nixon.

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

State Senate Minority Leader Rob Ortt, R-Niagara Falls, has been allotted one appointment following a recent retirement vacancy, according to the Albany Times-Union.

Fox News Digital reached out to Nixon via the Commission and the Senate Republicans’ office for comment.

The top Republican on the New York Senate Judiciary Committee told Fox News Digital that Nixon's pick should "alarm every New Yorker" that political activism appears to be "a requirement for consideration to serve on the bench."

"The appointment of a far-left, non-lawyer, Hollywood actress to the Commission on Judicial Nomination further reinforces the fact that the courts of New York have become entirely partisan," said Sen. Anthony Palumbo of Suffolk.

"We should all be outraged by the abuse of this branch of government that is supposed to be immune from politics."

With Nixon now serving on the commission, she will help evaluate and recommend future Court of Appeals nominees to the governor, a role that could allow progressive critics' objections to LaSalle to continue influencing the state's highest court long after that confirmation fight ended.

item.title: Fitness influencer Clavicular says he started taking testosterone at 14, calls natural training 'a waste'
item.description: Fitness influencer Clavicular told Sean Hannity he secretly ordered testosterone at age 14, calling natural weightlifting a "complete waste of time."
item.content:encoded:
Clavicular revealed on the "Hang Out with Sean Hannity" podcast that he abandoned natural weightlifting as a teenager in favor of taking testosterone.

On Tuesday's episode, the fitness and looksmaxxing personality – known for his controversial focus on "looksmaxxing" – called traditional training without performance-enhancing drugs a "complete waste of time."

Clavicular, whose real name is Braden Eric Peters, told Hannity that his interest in fitness began in his family's basement gym in Hoboken, New Jersey. After researching workout routines online, he decided that making progress naturally was too slow.

LOW TESTOSTERONE? HEGSETH SPARKS CONVERSATION AS DOCTORS REVEAL WARNING SIGNS EVERY MAN SHOULD KNOW

"I started reading different articles and forum posts about how to properly train as one would being a beginner to the gym, and that's sort of where I came to the conclusion that there's no reason to train naturally," Clavicular said.

"It's a complete waste of my time when I have this mechanism I could utilize, which was testosterone, to make the results in a much more efficient timeline. So why wouldn't I?"

The decision led Clavicular to secretly acquire performance-enhancing drugs at a young age, he shared.

"I used to have to camp out by the mailbox and pretend to be sick from school," he said, adding that he intercepted shipments of testosterone at age 14 to hide them from his parents.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE HEALTH STORIES

Hannity, a father of two, pointed out that many parents would react with anger and concern, prompting Clavicular to admit that his parents disciplined him repeatedly throughout his teenage years.

"Throughout high school, if I would do a f--- up, I would get sent [to my grandmother's]," he said. "Then, after I got expelled from university for peptides and 'roids, that's where I got sent off to."

Clavicular said his academic career came to an abrupt halt after his public social media posts about his steroid use caught up with him.

"Throughout my entire life, I've kind of been documenting everything in a pretty public way," he said. "I was talking about different cycles I was running, and then someone just sent the forum posts to my university, and it was done."

CLICK HERE TO SIGN UP FOR OUR HEALTH NEWSLETTER

While public discussions surrounding low testosterone levels and hormone health have grown in recent years – often focusing on symptoms like fatigue, low libido and loss of muscle mass – endocrinologists and medical experts consistently warn against nonmedical androgen use.

In adolescents and young adults, unsupervised anabolic steroid and peptide usage can carry severe health risks, including stunted skeletal growth, premature cardiovascular strain, liver damage, psychiatric mood alterations and irreversible fertility suppression, according to the Endocrine Society.

TEST YOURSELF WITH OUR LATEST LIFESTYLE QUIZ

Medical professionals emphasize that while hormone therapy can be beneficial for men with legitimate medical deficiencies, using testosterone as a shortcut for muscle growth bypasses essential metabolic and biological safeguards.

item.title: House Republican calls for yanking student visas of Chinese nationals
item.description: GOP Rep. Anna Paulina Luna of Florida is pressing the State Department to suspend or cancel the F-1 and J-1 visas of Chinese students studying in the U.S.
item.content:encoded:
Rep. Anna Paulina Luna, R-Fla., is pressing the State Department to yank F-1 and J-1 visas issued to Chinese students.

"I write today to urge the immediate suspension and cancellation of all F-1 and J-1 student visas held by Chinese nationals currently studying at institutions of higher education across the United States, considering recently declassified intelligence detailing the Chinese Communist Party's efforts to interfere in American elections," she wrote in a July 20 letter to Secretary of State Marco Rubio.

Fox News Digital reached out to the State Department on Tuesday: "We generally do not comment on congressional correspondence," a department spokesperson noted. "The Trump Administration is protecting our nation and its citizens by upholding the highest standards of national security and public safety through our visa process."

CHINA DISMISSES TRUMP ELECTION INTERFERENCE ACCUSATIONS AS ‘ENTIRELY FABRICATED,’ THREATENS COUNTERMEASURES

President Donald Trump delivered an address to the nation last week in which he said documents "show that over a period of years, starting during the 2020 election cycle, the People’s Republic of China carried out what is believed to be the largest compromise of election data in history — resulting in China’s illicit acquisition of 220 million U.S. voter files.

"That information includes names, addresses, phone numbers, political party preferences, and other sensitive data that would be needed to register to vote, and engage in other nefarious activities, which is exactly what was happening," he said.

TRUMP RELEASES DECLASSIFIED ELECTION INTELLIGENCE, SAYS IT REVEALS ‘SHOCKING VULNERABILITIES’

In her letter to Rubio, Luna wrote, "As I stated publicly following the President's address: every single Chinese student visa needs to be canceled immediately. 
This is a necessary and proportionate response to a documented state-sponsored effort to undermine the integrity of American elections."

‘SHADOW GOVERNMENT’: TRUMP CLAIMS INTEL COMMUNITY BRAGGED ABOUT HIDING CHINESE MEDDLING

"We urge the Department of State to immediately review and revoke all active Chinese national student visas, and to halt the issuance of new visas to Chinese nationals pending a full accounting of the extent of this infiltration and a comprehensive review of vetting procedures at our institutions of higher education," she wrote.

item.title: Chicago Cubs fan slaps younger fan for not standing during National Anthem in viral video
item.description: Viral video captures an older Chicago Cubs fan smacking a younger fan on the head for refusing to stand during the national anthem on Sunday.
item.content:encoded:
Who says patriotism is dead in this country?

America's 250th birthday has had plenty of moments where citizens have shown their country some love, but on Sunday afternoon, one patriot got a little too physical.

Before the Chicago Cubs and Minnesota Twins game, as the Jersey Girls were belting out a performance of "The Star Spangled Banner," fans rose to their feet, as is customary during the singing of our national anthem.

50 YEARS AGO TODAY, CUBS OUTFIELDER RICK MONDAY SAVED A BURNING AMERICAN FLAG AT DODGER STADIUM

As nearly everyone in attendance stood up and removed whatever headgear they were wearing, one fan remained seated, which didn't sit well with the man sitting directly behind him who decided to take matters into his own hands.

"Stand up," you can hear the older fan say to the younger one before he leans in and says something inaudible.

Then, he hauls off and smacks the kid right across the back of the head.

PETE CROW-ARMSTRONG GETS INTO UGLY, CURSE-FILLED SCREAMING MATCH WITH FEMALE WHITE SOX FAN

That must have worked, because the younger fan stood at attention for the rest of the performance.

And the older fan, to his credit, could be heard thanking the young man after he stands up (after telling him to "shut up," of course).

Now, look, I don't condone violence, but this is an all-timer of a video.

BLUE-HAIRED WORLD CUP FAN BREAKS SILENCE AFTER HIS EMOTIONAL REACTION GOES VIRAL

It doesn't even look like this is the first time our elder Cubs fan has pulled a move like this, either.

Or, at the very least, he's considered doing it and this is the first time he let his intrusive thoughts win out.

It seemed to be a fairly popular move, judging by the reactions in the comments section.

No one in the stadium seemed to be too opposed to the move either, other than the guy in the aviators who gave a half-hearted shrug and a few words when he saw the smack go down.

CLICK HERE FOR MORE SPORTS COVERAGE ON FOXNEWS.COM

SIGN UP TO GET THE LATEST SPORTS NEWS

It remains to be seen whether charges will be pressed, and if the kid wanted to he might be able to milk some legal action out of this.

But for now, it seems like all that will come of this is a hilarious viral moment fitting for America 250.

The smackings will continue until morale improves.

item.title: Trump faces Republican rebellion over Daylight Saving Time as Senate bill nears dead end
item.description: The Sunshine Protection Act passed the House, but Senate opposition from northern-state lawmakers over children going to school in the dark looms large.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump wants Congress to pass legislation that would give Americans a break from a twice-yearly nuisance, but the sun will likely set on the push in the Senate, where the issue is deeper than politics.

The House passed its bill, the Sunshine Protection Act, last week to allow states to voluntarily observe Daylight Saving Time year-round, but the legislation is hitting a brick wall in the Senate.

And it’s not the party aisle that’s defining the debate, it’s a horizontal line running through the United States that divides northern states versus southern states.

TIME ENOUGH AT LAST: WHAT'S NEXT AFTER THE HOUSE PASSES BILL TO DO AWAY WITH DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME?

"That is an issue in which there are passionate views on both sides," Senate Majority Leader John Thune, R-S.D., said on whether the Senate would consider the bill.

"So we'll see," he continued. "I mean, the House having acted on it now, we'll see what the interest level is in the Senate. But I know that there are people who are willing to really slow things down over that issue."

Proponents argue that it’s an inconvenience in their states and are hungry for more daylight in the evening, while opponents say that it would force school-aged children to be in the dark for the beginning of the day.

One of the most vocal opponents of the legislation is one of Trump’s own allies on Capitol Hill, Sen. Tom Cotton, R-Ark., who last year declared that he would "always oppose" efforts for permanent Daylight Saving Time.

HOUSE PASSES DAYLIGHT SAVING TIME REFORM AS TRUMP SIGNALS SUPPORT FOR ENDING CLOCK CHANGE

A source in Cotton’s orbit told Fox News Digital that the lawmaker was "far from the only one who objects" to the bill, and noted that the legislation is more of a grandfather clause that requires states to have already passed an observance of permanent standard time before the Sunshine Protection Act becomes law.

Hawaii and Arizona are the only two states that, by their own laws, observe permanent standard time, while no states observe a permanent Daylight Saving Time. It’s not the only time Congress has tried, either.

"Congress has already tried changing to permanent Daylight Saving Time before," the source said. "It was so thoroughly hated that Congress reversed course the following year — but almost no one knows that."

In the 1970s, lawmakers did enact a year-round Daylight Saving Time. But Americans rapidly soured on the change, especially parents whose children had to wait in the dark for the school bus. It was repealed about a year later.

GOP CIVIL WAR ERUPTS OVER TRUMP'S LATEST AGENDA PUSH AS KEY REPUBLICANS THREATEN TO SINK MEGABILL

"I can't support a motion to go to Daylight Saving Time, because it means that the kids in South Dakota ended up going to school in the dark for about the first hour of classes for more than six months out of a year," Sen. Mike Rounds, R-S.D., said. "So we can't do that."

Proponents argue, however, that it’s a long-needed change.

Sen. James Lankford, R-Okla., who originally introduced the Sunshine Protection Act alongside then-Sen. Marco Rubio, R-Fla., a decade ago, contended the push was "not a partisan issue, it’s a preference issue" that often fell along geographic lines.

Still, he acknowledged resistance from parents concerned about their kids going to school in the dark.

"This is left over from World War I when America was trying to save lamp oil. We're doing OK on lamp oil now, so that's not the issue on it," Lankford said. "With streetlights, with different power and things, we have — we're in a very different situation. And I talk to a lot of folks that say they are just sick of the time change. They want to be able to lock the clock."

And Sen. Rick Scott, R-Fla., who introduced the legislation last year, cheered the House passing the bill.

"It's one of the most popular things in my state," Scott said. "You wouldn't believe how much I get asked about this."

Still, he acknowledged the resistance coming from the North. He argued that he couldn’t understand how "anybody could vote against it when the state could make their own decision."

"Give me the chance — I mean, look, in my state, the ability, we want to do it," Scott said.

item.title: ‘Finish the damn job’: Hagerty tells 'Ruthless Podcast' that Trump will complete Iran mission
item.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty, R-Tenn., compared the current situation in the Middle East to President Harry Truman’s firing of Gen. Douglas MacArthur during the Korean War.
item.content:encoded:
Sen. Bill Hagerty, R-Tenn., said that President Donald Trump is on track to complete his military objectives in the war against Iran, during an interview with the "Ruthless Podcast." 

The former U.S. ambassador to Japan during Trump’s first term compared the current situation in the Middle East to President Harry Truman’s firing of Gen. Douglas MacArthur during the Korean War.

"I think about Douglas MacArthur in Japan," the senator said in an exclusive "Ruthless" interview released Tuesday morning. 

"I was fortunate to live in the same residence he occupied for six years when he was stationed there. He executed the Korean War after the rebuilding of Japan. I often wonder what would have happened if Truman had not fired him, if MacArthur had been allowed to finish the damn job," he added. "We wouldn't be dealing with a lot of the stuff that we're dealing with out there right now. And President Trump is going to finish that damn job."

PALANTIR CTO SAYS TECH INNOVATORS ARE BUILDING FOR AMERICA IN SHIFT SINCE TRUMP RETURNED TO WHITE HOUSE

Hagerty cited divine providence in saving Trump’s life as a guiding force to the administration’s approach to the current war. 

"You just brought me back to the Sunday morning after President Trump was shot in Butler, [Pennsylvania]," Hagerty said. "I was on the phone with him for about 20 minutes. I told him, 'You can be the most consequential president we've ever had,' and I think he's taken on that mantle; he realizes, but for the grace of God he wouldn't be here, and he's taking on the tough problems. He's not backing away from anything."

"Ruthless" co-host Josh Holmes shared Hagerty’s concerns about Iran gaining a nuclear weapon. 

"I think what I am most gratified by President Trump's decision to do it is he's the first president who decided ‘I'm not gonna leave this to another administration,’" Holmes said. "It's going to get worse. It's going to keep getting worse. You get October 7, you get the full funding of Hamas and Hezbollah. Who knows where it goes from there if they get a nuclear weapon and everything else. We already know their intentions. This is not something to be ignored."

CLICK HERE TO FOLLOW THE RUTHLESS PODCAST

Hagerty is seeking his first re-election after prevailing in the race for an open seat in 2020. No Democrat has won a U.S. Senate race in Tennessee since Al Gore in 1990.

The interview took place as part of the Ruthless Midterm Interview Series, an ongoing initiative to interview major candidates across the country. The hosts have already interviewed candidates in 17 states, with more scheduled ahead of the November midterms. 

The senator noted that the Democratic Party is rapidly headed left, especially on issues like Israel.

"[Senator] Bernie [Sanders (I-Vt.) is] in a position to declare himself the leader of the Democrat Party," Hagerty said. "Certainly in the United States Senate, that's the case. And I don't know who's leading over in the House. If it's not [Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez], you can tell me. But I think [House Minority Leader Hakeem] Jeffries doesn't seem to be delivering any leadership either. And if you look in Jeffries' own home state, it's just amazing how the Democrat socialists have taken over now."

FOLLOW FOX NEWS FLASH ON FACEBOOK

Voters in Tennessee head to the polls for the primaries on Aug. 6. The general election takes place on Nov. 3.

item.title: Dem ‘Epstein class’ attack backfires as top candidates funded by Epstein-tied billionaire
item.description: James Talarico and Jon Ossoff face scrutiny over major donations from Reid Hoffman, a billionaire with former associations to Jeffrey Epstein.
item.content:encoded:
Democratic Senate candidates James Talarico and Jon Ossoff are facing scrutiny over significant donations from Reid Hoffman, a billionaire mega-investor with former associations to Jeffrey Epstein, after both accused Republicans of representing the billionaire "Epstein class" over working Americans.

Both Talarico, running in Texas, and Ossoff, running in Georgia, have repeatedly criticized billionaires’ involvement in politics and charged Republicans with being beholden to the "Epstein class" instead of the American people.

Meanwhile, Hoffman, a co-founder of LinkedIn who has previously admitted to spending a night on Epstein’s Caribbean island, has heavily invested in both campaigns. In the Texas Senate race, Hoffman has donated a whopping $11.5 million to the Talarico-aligned super political action committee (PAC), Lone Star Rising, according to the Texas Tribune. The outlet said the billionaire investor sunk $10 million to the PAC in June alone, accounting for nearly 80% of the group’s second-quarter fundraising. In Georgia, Hoffman donated the maximum legal amount, $7,000, to Ossoff’s campaign for the 2025-2026 cycle.

"Democrats like Jon Ossoff and James Talarico vilify corporate billionaires in one breath and accept their cash in the next," a GOP operative remarked to Fox News Digital, adding, "They’re fueling the same ‘money in politics’ gravy train they claim to oppose."

TALARICO TOUTS TEXAS ROOTS AS OUT-OF-STATE CASH POWERS SENATE CAMPAIGN

Hoffman has not been charged with any wrongdoing related to his previous association with Epstein.

Their first known meeting was on July 19, 2013, almost five years after Epstein pled guilty in Florida to state charges of soliciting prostitution and procuring a minor for prostitution. According to Massachusetts Institute of Technology records, Hoffman, then a member of the university’s Media Lab Advisory Council, met with Epstein along with other faculty members on campus.

The next year, Hoffman visited Epstein’s private island, Little Saint James, and stayed overnight, the Wall Street Journal reported. Business Insider reported that in a recent podcast appearance, Hoffman admitted to staying one night at Epstein’s island as part of an MIT fundraising trip. Regarding his visit, he quipped, "Note to self: Google before going."

The Wall Street Journal reported that later in 2014, Hoffman sent Epstein ice cream, writing that it was for him or "for the girls."

'SHE SAW DOLLAR SIGNS': FORMER OBAMA COUNSEL PRESSED OVER YEARS-LONG EPSTEIN TIES

The two continued to maintain contact in the years that followed, with Hoffman and Epstein exchanging emails and Skype calls, according to the Wall Street Journal. Axios reported in 2019 that Hoffman hosted a dinner in 2015 at which Epstein was present along with other notable technology leaders, Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg and Peter Thiel.

The outlet reported that Hoffman apologized for his association with Epstein, saying that "by agreeing to participate in any fundraising activity where Epstein was present, I helped to repair his reputation and perpetuate injustice. For this, I am deeply regretful."

Meanwhile, Talarico has repeatedly criticized Republicans by using the "Epstein class" line. In one interview, he asserted, "The Epstein Class has no place in Texas."

On the campaign trail, Forbes reported Talarico charging that "the only minority destroying America is the billionaires." His campaign website asserts that "billionaire mega-donors aren't just buying yachts and jets — they're buying power, and they're using that power to rig our political system."

Fox News Digital reached out to Lone Star Rising, which has spent millions on pro-Talarico advertising, for comment on Hoffman’s $11.5 million in donations.

JT Ennis, a spokesperson for Talarico’s campaign, remarked to Fox News Digital that "our campaign has no control over contributions to any super PAC," adding, "federal law prohibits coordination between political campaigns and outside entities."

Ennis said that "anyone contributing to this movement is supporting a campaign that will tax billionaires more and limit their outsized political influence."

Meanwhile, he ripped into Talarico’s Republican opponent, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton, saying, "The only way to get Big Money out of our politics is to reject politicians like Ken Paxton," who he accused of "want[ing] corporations and billionaires to decide our elections, not Texans."

TALARICO SAYS THE DIVIDE IS 'TOP VS. BOTTOM' — THEN HEADS TO ONE OF AMERICA'S RICHEST DONOR ENCLAVES

"While Ken Paxton works to enrich himself and his donors, James will remain the only candidate in this race fighting to ban super PACs, ban corporate PACs, ban congressional stock trading, and tax billionaires so we can fix this broken, corrupt political system," said Ennis.

Fox News Digital reached out to Paxton’s campaign for comment.

Fox News Digital also reached out to the campaign for Ossoff, who criticized the Trump administration in a news statement earlier this year for having the "wealthiest Cabinet ever."

SIGN UP TO GET THE POLITICS NEWSLETTER

Ossoff remarked in the statement, "We were told that MAGA was for working-class Americans. But this is a government of, by, and for the ultra-rich... this is the Epstein class, ruling our country. They are the elites they pretend to hate."

On the campaign trail, Ossoff has railed against American politics for "running on secret money, corporate money, billionaire money" and accused the Trump administration of taking "American corruption to spectacular new heights."

Besides Talarico and Ossoff, Hoffman is also known to have donated the maximum legal amount, $7,000, to the re-election campaign for Sen. Cory Booker, a prominent national Democrat from New Jersey.

Fox News Digital reached out to Booker for comment.

item.title: Cuba built covert network inside America through spies, activists and universities, State Dept alleges
item.description: The 100-page report alleges Cuban intelligence infiltrated the State Department, National Security Council and Pentagon over nearly seven decades.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba spent decades waging a covert campaign to infiltrate the United States through espionage and ideological influence, according to a new State Department report that calls Havana the "capital of 21st century communism."

Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the report is intended to expose what he described as Havana's decades-long campaign against the United States.

"For more than six decades, the Cuban regime has been the leading sponsor of radical leftism and Third Worldism in the United States," Rubio wrote on X. "The State Department is exposing the full history of Cuban espionage and subversion in our country. The American people deserve to know."

TRUMP EXPANDS CUBA SANCTIONS BEYOND US COMPANIES IN MAJOR CRACKDOWN ON FOREIGN ENABLERS

Cuba's foreign minister, Bruno Rodríguez Parrilla, dismissed the report as political propaganda.

In a statement posted on X, Rodríguez called it the State Department's "latest fallacy" about Cuba and accused the Trump administration of manufacturing a pretext to continue what he described as Washington's "cruel and criminal economic war" against the island. Without naming Rubio, he accused "that U.S. politician from South Florida" of concocting a "macabre plan" that advances only the interests "of those who have always paid him."

The 100-page report argues that Cuba's campaign reached far beyond traditional espionage. It alleges that Havana cultivated American activists, recruited spies, supported revolutionary movements and built relationships with universities and advocacy groups as part of a long-term effort to undermine U.S. interests and spread communist ideology.

"For nearly seven decades, the Cuban regime has waged a sustained campaign of subversion against the United States," the report says. It alleges Cuban intelligence infiltrated "the top levels of the State Department, the National Security Council, and the Pentagon," while also helping shape generations of left-wing political movements.

INSIDE CUBA’S FOREIGN INFLUENCE CAMPAIGN: FROM THE '60S VENCEREMOS BRIGADE TO A CALIFORNIA UNION HALL

The report further alleges that Havana spent decades cultivating ties to American revolutionary and activist groups, including the Weather Underground, the Black Panthers, the Black Liberation Army and Puerto Rican militant organizations. The report claims that this network later expanded to include connections with Antifa, anti-ICE organizations, socialist groups and left-wing nonprofits.

The report argues that Cuba's greatest success came not through military force but through influence. It says the communist government built an international network of activists, academics, nonprofits and political organizations that continued spreading its revolutionary ideology long after the Soviet Union collapsed.

The report points to several high-profile espionage cases, including convicted spies Victor Manuel Rocha, Ana Belén Montes, Walter Kendall Myers and members of the Wasp Network.

ALLEGED CUBAN INFLUENCE OPERATIVE, FAMILY IN FEDERAL CUSTODY AFTER RUBIO REVOKES LEGAL STATUS

It also cites former CIA counterintelligence chief James Olson, who called Cuba's intelligence service "probably the most aggravating intelligence service I've ever worked against," and warned, "The Cubans have penetrated our government… We only know the tip of the iceberg."

The report also claims Cuba viewed American colleges and universities as recruiting grounds, identifying politically sympathetic students who could later move into influential jobs in government or the private sector. It suggests the partnerships between the University of Havana and dozens of U.S. universities are examples of what it describes as long-term influence efforts.

It also revisits Cuba's ties to revolutionary movements across Latin America and says Havana provided sanctuary to American fugitives tied to militant groups. The report names organizations including the Venceremos Brigade, ICAP, the National Network on Cuba, the Democratic Socialists of America, Code Pink and the National Lawyers Guild, arguing they helped maintain pro-Cuba activism in the United States for decades.

The report concludes that Cuba evolved from a Soviet ally into what it calls "the ideological capital of the modern radical left," arguing the regime increasingly relied on espionage, political organizing and influence campaigns instead of conventional military power.

Fox News Digital reached out to the Cuban Embassy in the U.S. for further comment but did not immediately hear back.

Earlier this month, U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz appeared on Fox News to warn that Cuba remains a national security threat and accused China and Russia of collecting information around U.S. military bases in Cuba.

"The Cuban regime is not only a threat to its own people, it's a national security threat, and this administration is not going to stand for it any longer," Waltz said.

item.title: Dolphins cheerleaders make major moves to compete with Cowboys cheerleaders, Tebows get out & Ryan Clark!
item.description: Ryan Clark's ESPN departure signals a shift away from the post-Floyd era, while the Miami Dolphins cheerleaders make a bold World Cup statement.
item.content:encoded:
We get the day rolling around here at OutKick with a fresh, new edition of Tuesday Screencaps with Ryan Clark's ESPN career coming to a close, storms overhead and the Dolphins cheerleaders getting dialed in early for the 2026 season.

Let's start with Clark's departure, as reported by the OutKick Big Js who came together for one of the most explosive reports of the year. If you go back to when we started the OutKick Woke All-Star Challenge like six years ago, it was at the height of the post-George Floyd era when lunatic LIB hot take artists faced zero pushback over where they were taking this country.

In 2022, OutKick's Bobby Burack reported on Screamin' A. Smith, Clark and how ESPN allowed those two to dictate editorial direction post-Floyd.

ESPN PARTING WAYS WITH NFL ANALYST RYAN CLARK AHEAD OF NETWORK'S FIRST SUPER BOWL BROADCAST

Based on what happened Monday, it appears ESPN's pivoting (not all the way) out of that post-Floyd era when our Woke All-Star Challenges were loaded with ESPN talent. Will we notice a new direction with ESPN shows? Time will tell. Something tells me Clark will be replaced with younger, cheaper talent that will keep the wokes happy.

Moving along, I want to address the offseason turned in by the Miami Dolphins cheerleaders. Normally, NFL cheerleader groups are rather quiet during the summer. There might be content from tryouts and the requisite 'Happy Birthday' posts, but this summer, the Dolphins cheerleaders made a bold statement during the World Cup.

The Dallas Cowboys cheerleaders officially have competition on the content front which is hard to even fathom. But there were the Dolphins cheerleaders at multiple World Cup games, including the bronze medal game, keeping the fans fired up.

Add in the news that the team chose viral Masters Girl to the squad and you have the makings of a team to watch this NFL season. Who is Masters Girl? That would be Aaliyah Kikumoto, whose father, Charles, is a BIG OutKick fan. Charles even flew in two summers ago to win the 2-Club Challenge golf outing that I used to throw.

This is how you raise the team profile to compete with the Cowboys. Own Page Six. Get the squad into Screencaps/Nightcaps. Now we have ourselves a real battle.

📩 Email: joe.kinsey@outkick.com Send photos, stories, tips, rants—whatever you've got.

KRISTIN CAVALLARI LETS LOOSE DURING A DOMINANT MIAMI SWIM WEEK, ESPN'S VULGAR HOT MIC & 'BAMA BEER WALL!

🐦 Twitter/X: @JoeKinseyexp Tag me or drop a DM.

👥 Facebook Group: Join the Screencaps Community
